title: bridge_health.sh -A -L — wire atlas_alert_subscriber into the cron pipeline
summary: Add an opt-in --alert-check / -L flag to tool/bridge_health.sh that, after the existing -A anomaly check, invokes tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa via 'hexa run' (CLI-only) and forwards its __ATLAS_ALERT__ sentinel(s) to stdout. One single CLI invocation now drives the full health → anomaly → alert pipeline; cron/launchd no longer needs a second job. Symmetric with the existing -A / -F flag pattern, no new files created.

predecessors.omega_bridge_15: wired bridge_health.sh to append per-bridge rows + opt-in anomaly auto-invoke (-A)
predecessors.omega_bridge_16: promoted __BRIDGE_ANOMALY__ sentinel into a structured sibling JSONL row scoped 'bridge_anomaly'
predecessors.omega_bridge_17: built tool/atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa — JSONL-stream subscriber that emits __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED|PASS|SKIP

context.problem: After ω-bridge-17, the alert subscriber existed as a separate tool but nothing in the pipeline auto-invoked it. An operator (or a separate cron entry) had to remember to run it after each sweep. Two-step manual orchestration violates the single-CLI-entrypoint principle that the -A flag established.

design_alternatives_considered.rejected_because: adds a third tool to maintain; orchestrator surface area is wasted for a 2-step linear chain

design_alternatives_considered.chosen_because: minimal new surface; symmetric with existing -A / -F pattern; CLI-only invocation (.sh hexa-runs the .hexa); no new files

edits: header comment block: added --alert-check / -L usage lines + ω-bridge-18 origin paragraph
--help sed range bumped from 3,47 → 3,58 to include the new origin block
unknown-flag fix line extended to mention --alert-check

post-anomaly block: ALERT_WARN_THR / ALERT_INSUF_THR / ALERT_LAST_N env-overridable defaults (0.4 / 0.6 / 10) + HEXA_ARGV='--quiet --timeline $TIMELINE --last $N --warn-threshold $WT --insufficient-threshold $IT' invocation of atlas_alert_subscriber.hexa, stdout forwarded verbatim, no JSONL append (raw 71/77 read-only on $TIMELINE)

sentinels_now_emitted_by_bridge_health_s: __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=warn_ratio ratio=R threshold=T last_n=N sampled=S
__ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=insufficient_ratio ratio=R threshold=T last_n=N sampled=S
__ATLAS_ALERT__ SKIP reason=subscriber_no_output path=... (bridge_health.sh local skip on empty stdout)

exit_code_semantics.rule: alert firing does NOT change the script exit code (raw 71 report-only)
exit_code_semantics.rationale: alerts are derived diagnostics over a trend window; the immediate sweep already encoded its verdict via __BRIDGE_HEALTH__. Mixing trend-alert exit codes into the per-sweep return would conflate per-sweep error signaling with longitudinal trend signaling and break cron alerting that keys off ec=76.

compliance.raw_9_hexa_only_suffix: no new files; existing tracked .sh (bridge_health.sh) grandfathered for edits per ω-bridge-15/16/17 precedent
compliance.raw_71_report_only: alert firing is purely diagnostic; never alters script exit code
compliance.raw_77_append_only: ALERT_CHECK block does NOT append to $TIMELINE — read-only consumer; only bridge-15 (history TSV) and bridge-16 (anomaly JSONL row) write
compliance.raw_80_sentinel: all five __ATLAS_ALERT__ shapes preserved verbatim from subscriber

help_visibility.result: shows --alert-check, -L, and the ω-bridge-18 origin block

unknown_flag.result: ec=1, fix-line lists --alert-check among allowed flags

argparse_isolated.method: extracted lines 87-112 (decls + while-case-done) into a shell function, called with various flag combinations

subscriber_direct.stdout: __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=warn_ratio ratio=0.8000 threshold=0.4 last_n=10 sampled=5

alert_block_extracted.method: extracted bridge_health.sh lines 424-449 (the new ALERT_CHECK block) and ran with TIMELINE=/tmp/omega_bridge_18_fixture_timeline.jsonl ALERT_CHECK=1
results: high-WARN fixture (4/5): __ATLAS_ALERT__ FIRED rule=warn_ratio ratio=0.8000 threshold=0.4 last_n=10 sampled=5
benign fixture (PASS only): __ATLAS_ALERT__ PASS sampled=2 rules_evaluated=2

no_jsonl_writes_to_dirty_timeline.audit: grep '>>' on the new block (lines 424-449) returned no matches; redirections in the file are limited to L155 (BRIDGE_HISTORY_TSV — owned by ω-bridge-15) and L355/L416 (TIMELINE writes from bridge_registry summary and bridge_anomaly sibling row — both pre-existing and gated by the JSON / ANOMALY_CHECK flags respectively)
no_jsonl_writes_to_dirty_timeline.result: ω-bridge-18 ALERT_CHECK block is provably read-only on $TIMELINE

operational_notes.cron_invocation: bash tool/bridge_health.sh -A -L --quiet (or -A -L -F --quiet for the full triad: anomaly + alert + fingerprint)
operational_notes.back_compat: all existing invocations continue to work; -L is opt-in OFF by default
operational_notes.non_collision_with_parallel_sessions: no edits to cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own; only tool/bridge_health.sh

future_omega_bridge_19_candidates: tail-mode subscriber (continuous, watches $TIMELINE for appends rather than one-shot poll)
alert dispatch integration (slack/email/pushover via webhook on FIRED)
rule expansion: streak-based (N consecutive WARNs), exponential-backoff suppression for repeat fires
